reasoning: The development and approval of a new vaccine typically takes years, and currently, there are no approved fungal vaccines for humans. Scientific literature from late 2025 (e.g., Journal of Clinical Investigation, November 2025) explicitly states that fungal vaccines are 'still far off' and candidates for Blastomycosis are only in the early preclinical stages (tested in mice and dogs), with no ongoing human phase clinical trials. Because there is no vaccine close to approval, the scientific reality will not change within the 1-month forecast horizon. It is highly improbable that a new vaccine will be developed and subsequently added to the Wikipedia 'List of infectious diseases' by either January 25, 2026, or February 17, 2026. Given the rules require a minimum probability of 0.02 and increasing uncertainty over time, I am assigning a 2% and 3% probability for the two resolution dates respectively, covering the remote possibility of vandalism or an uncorrected erroneous edit on the Wikipedia page on the exact resolution days.
